workflow.name: Send a curated tech RSS digest to Gmail with OpenAI GPT-5 Mini
workflow.description:
# Workflow: RSS to E-Mail
*7 Nodes + AI Model
*

### ⚙️ Pipeline Steps
1. Daily Morning Trigger
Fires every day at 8:00 AM.

2. Define RSS Sources
Outputs 27 curated RSS feed URLs across 7 categories:

Category --------Sources
![image.png](fileId:5431)

3. Fetch RSS Feeds
Reads each feed (includes error handling so one broken feed won't kill the workflow).

4. Clean and Prepare for AI
Deduplicates by URL, strips HTML tags, truncates descriptions, sorts by date, and keeps the 150 most recent articles.

5. AI Article Curator (GPT-5 Mini)
Reviews all articles and picks the 30 most interesting and diverse ones.

6. Build Digest Email
Generates a beautiful HTML email with a purple gradient header, numbered article cards with titles, descriptions, dates, and source attribution.

7. Send Digest Email
Sends the final curated digest via Gmail.


### 🚀 How to Activate
To get this workflow running, you need to:

	• Connect OpenAI: Add your credentials to the AI model node.

	• Connect Gmail: Add your credentials to the Send node.

	• Set Target Email: Update your email address in the "Send Digest Email" node (it is currently a placeholder).

Enable: Toggle the workflow to Active.

![image.png](fileId:5432)

parameters.jsCode:
var feeds = [
  { url: "http://news.ycombinator.com/rss", source: "Hacker News" },
  { url: "https://techcrunch.com/feed/", source: "TechCrunch" },
  { url: "http://www.theverge.com/rss/full.xml", source: "The Verge" },
  { url: "http://venturebeat.com/feed/", source: "VentureBeat" },
  { url: "https://blog.pragmaticengineer.com/rss/", source: "The Pragmatic Engineer" },
  { url: "https://engineering.fb.com/feed/", source: "Engineering at Meta" },
  { url: "https://engineering.atspotify.com/feed/", source: "Spotify Engineering" },
  { url: "https://stripe.com/blog/feed.rss", source: "Stripe Blog" },
  { url: "https://slack.engineering/feed", source: "Slack Engineering" },
  { url: "https://blog.cloudflare.com/rss/", source: "Cloudflare Blog" },
  { url: "https://shopifyengineering.myshopify.com/blogs/engineering.atom", source: "Shopify Engineering" },
  { url: "https://openai.com/news/engineering/rss.xml", source: "OpenAI" },
  { url: "http://googleresearch.blogspot.com/atom.xml", source: "Google AI Blog" },
  { url: "https://deepmind.com/blog/feed/basic/", source: "DeepMind" },
  { url: "http://news.mit.edu/rss/topic/artificial-intelligence2", source: "MIT News AI" },
  { url: "https://www.amazon.science/index.rss", source: "Amazon Science" },
  { url: "https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/feed", source: "NVIDIA Developer" },
  { url: "http://firstround.com/review/feed.xml", source: "First Round Review" },
  { url: "https://lethain.com/feeds/", source: "Irrational Exuberance" },
  { url: "http://www.producthunt.com/feed", source: "Product Hunt" },
  { url: "http://www.quantamagazine.org/feed/", source: "Quanta Magazine" },
  { url: "https://www.sciencealert.com/rss", source: "ScienceAlert" },
  { url: "https://singularityhub.com/feed/", source: "Singularity Hub" },
  { url: "https://jvns.ca/atom.xml", source: "Julia Evans" },
  { url: "https://danluu.com/atom.xml", source: "Dan Luu" },
  { url: "https://overreacted.io/rss.xml", source: "Dan Abramov" },
  { url: "https://www.joelonsoftware.com/feed/", source: "Joel on Software" }
];
return feeds.map(function(f) { return { json: f }; });

parameters.text:
=I have {{ $json.articleCount }} articles from various tech RSS feeds today. Please select the 30 most interesting, impactful, and diverse articles.

Here are all the articles:

{{ $json.articleList }}

Return ONLY a valid JSON object with this exact structure: {"selected": [0, 5, 12, ...]} where the numbers are the article indices from the list above.

Selection criteria:
1. Timely and newsworthy
2. Diverse topics (AI, engineering, startups, science, design)
3. Genuinely interesting to a tech professional
4. Avoid duplicates or very similar stories
5. Prioritize original insights over press releases

options.systemMessage: You are an expert tech news curator. Your job is to review a list of articles from various RSS feeds and select exactly 30 of the most interesting and diverse ones for a daily digest email. You must return ONLY valid JSON with the selected article indices in this format: {"selected": [index1, index2, ...]}. Do not include any other text, explanation, or formatting.

parameters.content:
Workflow: RSS to E-Mail (7 nodes + AI model)


1. Daily Morning Trigger - Fires every day at 8:00 AM

2. Define RSS Sources - Outputs 27 curated RSS feed URLs across 7 categories:

Tech News:
 Hacker News, TechCrunch, The Verge, VentureBeat
Engineering: Meta, Spotify, Stripe, Slack, Cloudflare, Shopify, The Pragmatic Engineer
AI & ML: OpenAI, Google AI, DeepMind, MIT News, Amazon Science, NVIDIA

Startup: 
First Round Review, Irrational Exuberance
Products: Product Hunt

Science: 
Quanta Magazine, ScienceAlert, Singularity Hub

Bloggers:
Julia Evans, Dan Luu, Dan Abramov, Joel on Software
3. Fetch RSS Feeds - Reads each feed (with error handling so one broken feed won't kill the workflow)

4. Clean and Prepare for AI - Deduplicates by URL, strips HTML tags, truncates descriptions, sorts by date, keeps the 150 most recent articles

5. AI Article Curator (GPT-5 Mini) - Reviews all articles and picks the 30 most interesting/diverse ones

6. Build Digest Email - Generates a beautiful HTML email with a purple gradient header, numbered article cards with titles, descriptions, dates, and source attribution

7. Send Digest Email - Sends via Gmail

To activate it, you need to:

Connect your OpenAI credentials to the AI model node
Connect your Gmail credentials to the Send node
Set your email address in the "Send Digest Email" node (it's a placeholder right now)
Toggle the workflow to Active

1.Daily Morning Trigger Fires every day at 8:00 AM


2. Define RSS Sources - Outputs 27 curated RSS feed URLs across 7 categories

3.Fetch RSS Feeds - Reads each feed (with error handling so one broken feed won't kill the workflow)


4. Clean and Prepare for AI - Deduplicates by URL, strips HTML tags, truncates descriptions, sorts by date, keeps the 150 most recent articles
5.Article Curator (GPT-5 Mini) - Reviews all articles and picks the 30 most interesting/diverse ones

6.Build Digest Email - Generates a beautiful HTML email with a purple gradient header, numbered article cards with titles, descriptions, dates, and source attribution

7.Send Digest Email - Sends via Gmail
